evals.prompt: Audit the CSS architecture of this project and produce a full assessment report
evals.expected_output: Structured report with layer strategy, token audit, specificity findings, dark mode readiness, and remediation plan

assertions.text: Output contains evidence tags [HECHO], [INFERENCIA], or [SUPUESTO]
Output contains TL;DR section
Output mentions !important count or specificity analysis
Output mentions CSS custom properties or design tokens
Output mentions @layer or cascade layer strategy
Output provides priority-sorted remediation (P1/P2/P3)
Output includes effort estimate for recommendations
Output identifies at least one anti-pattern from the skill definition
Output does not fabricate file paths not confirmed with scan
Output references scanning commands or evidence sources

evals.prompt: Design a design token system for a React application with dark mode and multi-brand theming
evals.expected_output: Three-tier token architecture with primitives, semantic tokens, component tokens, and dark mode strategy

assertions.text: Output defines primitive tokens (raw values)
Output defines semantic tokens (meaningful names)
Output separates primitive and semantic token layers
Output provides dark mode override strategy using [data-theme] or @media
Output mentions color-scheme property for native elements
Output includes CSS custom property syntax (-- prefix)
Semantic tokens reference primitive tokens via var()
Output mentions Style Dictionary for multi-platform if multi-brand
Output provides concrete token file structure

evals.prompt: Migrate an existing codebase from Tailwind CSS v3 to Tailwind v4
evals.expected_output: Migration plan with @theme configuration, breaking changes, and verification steps

assertions.text: Output mentions reading tailwind.config.js first [HECHO]
Output explains @theme directive in CSS replacing tailwind.config.js
Output mentions @source replacing content array
Output identifies breaking changes
Output provides concrete @theme CSS block example
Output provides verification/testing steps

evals.prompt: Implement CSS @scope for component isolation in a design system
evals.expected_output: Implementation of @scope with root boundary, lower boundary, and comparison to CSS Modules

assertions.text: Output uses @scope syntax correctly
Output explains :scope pseudo-class within @scope
Output mentions browser support (Chrome 118+, Firefox 128+)
Output compares @scope to CSS Modules as alternative
Output provides lower boundary syntax example
Output tags browser support as risk or assumption if project has older support targets

evals.prompt: There are 847 !important declarations in this codebase. Design a migration strategy to CSS Layers
evals.expected_output: Phased migration strategy from !important-heavy CSS to CSS Layers without breaking the application

assertions.text: Output reads the codebase before proposing
Output defines the layer order with @layer statement
Output provides phased migration plan (not one big bang change)
Output explains how to wrap third-party CSS in layers
Output provides testing strategy to verify no regressions
Output identifies which !important uses can be eliminated vs. which need investigation
